views.markdown:
**Download anything, hide the noise, browse invisibly** — in Arabic or English.

Albrhi puts a download button where Instagram should have put one, strips the feed back to the people you actually follow, and stops the app reporting what you watch.

Tested on Instagram **410.1.0** — the newest build the developer's phone will still accept. Nothing here is pinned to a version number, so newer builds should work. If one misbehaves, Settings › Diagnostics writes the bug report for you.

A native download button in the action row of every post and reel, beside the save icon. One tap takes the highest quality available straight to Photos. Stories and view-once DMs have their own button, and reels can be saved as video or as the original audio.

A real queue: pause, resume, retry, several transfers at once, and downloads that keep running after you leave the app. Duplicates are detected, finished files are cleared once they reach Photos, and a searchable history records everything you saved.
Remove ads, sponsored and suggested posts, suggested accounts and reels, Threads posts, Meta AI, the friends map, trending searches and the explore grid. Hide the stories tray, or the entire feed.

Watch stories without sending a seen receipt, and choose per story when to send one. View-once photos and videos stay unseen until you tap the eye button on that exact message. Hide the typing indicator and disable screenshot detection.
An optional prompt before liking, following, reposting, calling, commenting or sending a voice message — so a mis-tap never turns into a notification.
Full Arabic and English with automatic right-to-left layout, SF Symbols, a customisable accent colour and dark mode. Hold the menu button on your profile to open it.

## v3.1.8.4

- **Experimental: 1080p downloads via on-device transcoding.** Instagram serves
  its high-quality video as AV1, which iOS cannot save. Turn on "Transcode AV1 to
  1080p" in Downloads and Albrhi re-encodes it to H.264 on the device — no server,
  nothing sent anywhere. It is heavier on battery and slower, so it is off by
  default; when off, or if a transcode fails, downloads behave exactly as before.

## v3.1.8.3

- Video downloads now read Instagram's full DASH quality ladder, not just the
  single ready-made rendition. When a higher H.264/HEVC version is available it
  is taken automatically; otherwise nothing changes. Groundwork for saving the
  AV1-only qualities is in place but not yet switched on.

## v3.1.8.2

- The DASH diagnostic from 3.1.8.1 came back empty because it questioned only
  the video object, and because the names it looked for were guesses. It now
  asks the media object too, and asks the runtime which names exist instead of
  assuming. Diagnostics only — nothing else changes.
_Free and open source. Not affiliated with, endorsed by or sponsored by Instagram or Meta Platforms._
